name: Nsc (Naca Counseling Subsidiary) - Jackson, Ms
description_short: NACA offers character-based mortgage lending with no down payment, no closing costs, and no credit score consideration. A non-profit focused on closing the racial wealth gap through affordable homeownership.
description_long:
The Neighborhood Assistance Corporation of America (NACA) was founded in 1988 by the Boston Hotel Workers Union as the first housing trust fund initiative, evolving into a national non-profit organization dedicated to expanding homeownership access. Over 36 years, NACA has served approximately 3 million people and helped over 500,000 become homeowners through character-based lending practices that prioritize borrower circumstances over credit scores.

NACA's primary service is mortgage lending with distinctive terms: no down payment required, no closing costs or fees, no mortgage insurance premiums, and rates that don't factor in credit scores. As of April 2026, they offer fixed rates of 5.625% for 30-year mortgages, 5.125% for 20-year, and 5% for 15-year terms. They also provide HUD-approved housing counseling (30% of all HUD housing counseling nationally), hold "Achieve the Dream" multi-day events across the country to qualify borrowers, operate a member portal for document uploads and status tracking, and fight predatory landlords and corporate real estate investors through advocacy campaigns.

NACA distinguishes itself through character-based lending that explicitly does not consider credit scores, positioning themselves as an alternative to traditional mortgage lenders. They've made $20 billion in mortgage commitments and originated 75,000 NACA mortgages. Their mission explicitly targets closing the racial wealth disparity gap, serving low- to moderate-income people and communities of color historically excluded from homeownership. They combine lending with activism, volunteer opportunities (NACtivist program), and community organizing against predatory housing practices.

The main caveat is that NACA appears to operate primarily through multi-day "Achieve the Dream" events rather than year-round local offices, suggesting access may require travel or attendance at scheduled regional events. The Jackson, MS location referenced in the company name appears to be a subsidiary office, but the website emphasizes a national footprint with events scheduled in Arkansas, Wisconsin, Florida, and Connecticut. Prospective borrowers must become "NACA Qualified" through their event process before mortgage access.
pros: No down payment required for mortgages, removing the largest barrier to homeownership for many borrowers
No closing costs or fees and no mortgage insurance, significantly reducing total homeownership costs
Character-based lending that explicitly does not consider credit scores, helping borrowers with poor credit histories
Below-market fixed rates (5.625% 30-year as of April 2026) competitive with traditional lenders despite serving high-risk demographics
HUD-approved housing counseling provided (30% of all national HUD counseling), offering education beyond lending
$20 billion in mortgage commitments and 75,000 mortgages originated demonstrate scale and stability
Explicit social mission targeting racial wealth gap and serving low-to-moderate income and communities of color
cons: Limited local presence—appears to operate primarily through multi-day regional "Achieve the Dream" events rather than year-round offices, requiring potential travel
Website lacks detailed eligibility criteria, loan limits, property type restrictions, or application timeline information
No clear information on geographic service area or whether all locations are equally accessible
Character-based lending criteria undefined—unclear what specific factors they evaluate beyond credit score
Member portal login required to access detailed information about applying or checking status, creating barriers to preliminary inquiry
best_for: Low-to-moderate income borrowers with poor or no credit history seeking first-time homeownership
Communities of color and historically underserved populations facing traditional lending discrimination
Borrowers who cannot save a down payment or afford closing costs and mortgage insurance premiums
Borrowers willing to attend multi-day events in exchange for below-market rates and character-based consideration
diagnosis: NACA is best for low-to-moderate income borrowers and communities of color with poor credit seeking affordable homeownership without down payments or closing costs. The primary caveat is that access requires attending multi-day regional "Achieve the Dream" events to become NACA Qualified, rather than year-round local office applications, and the Jackson, MS location appears to be a subsidiary rather than a primary service center.
services: Character-based mortgage lending with no down payment
Fixed-rate mortgages (15, 20, and 30-year terms) at below-market rates
Mortgage origination with no closing costs or fees
Multi-day "Achieve the Dream" qualifying events across regions
Member portal for document submission and application status tracking
Advocacy and campaigns against predatory landlords and corporate real estate investors
NACtivist volunteer program and community organizing opportunities
Mortgage servicing and member support through portal
Rental experience reporting for predatory landlord documentation
Financial donation and fundraising support for organizational mission
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 6424 Lakeover Rd Ste B, Jackson, MS 39213
